steps.text: Quantify isomers meeting identification criteria in Section 11.4.3.4 of Method 23 under paragraph (g)(2), regardless of whether the isomers meet identification criteria in Section 11.4.3.4.1 of Method 23.
Quantify the isomers per Section 11.4.3.5 of Method 23 under paragraph (g)(2).
Quantify isomers meeting identification criteria in Section 11.4.3.4 of Method 23 under paragraph (j)(2), regardless of whether the isomers meet identification Section 11.4.3.4.1 of Method 23.
Quantify the isomers per Section 11.4.3.5 of Method 23 under paragraph (j)(2).

Use column systems for measuring PAH that can achieve separation of anthracene and phenanthrene at m/z 178 such that the valley between the peaks does not exceed 50% of the taller of the two peaks, and benzo[b]fluoranthene and benzo[k]fluoranthene such that the valley between the peaks is less than 60% of the height of the taller peak.
Use column systems for measuring PCB that can achieve unique resolution and identification of the toxics for determination of a TEQPCB using toxic equivalency factors (TEF).
Soak all glassware in hot soapy water (Alconox® or equivalent).
Rinse with hot tap water.
Rinse with deionized/distilled water.
Rinse with methanol.
Rinse with toluene.
Bake glassware up to 400 °C (752 °F) for a minimum of 2 hours if necessary to remove contaminants or interferents.
Allow glassware to cool after baking.
Cover glassware openings with clean glass fitting caps or cleaned aluminum foil.
Rinse glassware immediately before use with acetone and toluene.
Load the modules in a clean area.
Fill a module with 20 to 40 g of XAD–2.
Spike modules before the sampling event.
Add the pre-sampling adsorbent standard to the top quarter of the adsorbent bed rather than onto the top or bottom of the adsorbent bed.
Add sufficient spike (picograms (pg)/module) to result in the final sample theoretical concentrations specified in Tables 23–7, 23–8, and 23–9.
Follow the XAD–2 with cleaned glass wool and tightly cap both ends of the module.
Inspect the train prior to and during disassembly.
Note and record any abnormal conditions (e.g., broken filters, colored impinger liquid).
Recover and prepare samples for shipping as follows in Sections 8.2.4 through 8.2.12 of this method.

Identify and quantify specific target compounds or isomers that do not have corresponding pre-extraction standard compounds by comparing to the pre-extraction standard of the same compound class with the nearest retention time to target compound.
Calculate the response factor of each target native compound relative to its labeled pre-extraction standard analog using Equation 23-1.
Use Equation 23–2 to calculate the RRF for pre-extraction standard.
Calculate Average RRF for Each Compound Over the Minimum of Five Calibration Levels (12.3).
Calculate Percent RSD of the RRFs for a Compound Over the Calibration Levels (12.4).
Calculate Standard Deviation of the RRFs for a Compound Over the Calibration Levels (12.5).
Calculate Percent Difference of the RRF of the Continuing Calibration Verification Compared to the Average RRF from the Initial Calibration for Each Target Compound (12.6).
Dry the adsorbent of extraction solvent before use.
Connect the gas vent of a liquid nitrogen cylinder or the exit of the activated carbon scrubber to the column by a length of precleaned copper tubing coiled to pass through a heat source.
Allow the methylene chloride to drain from the resin.
Place the resin in the drying apparatus.
Flow nitrogen through the drying apparatus at a rate that does not fluidize or agitate the resin.
Continue the nitrogen flow until the residual solvent is removed.

preconditions.text: Glassware must be cleaned thoroughly before using.
Do not spike the modules in the field.
For analysis that includes PAH, use spiked modules within 14 days.
The retention time for the analytes must be within 3 seconds of the corresponding labeled pre-extraction standard.

The signals for the two exact masses in Tables 23–4 and 23–6 of this method for PCDD/PCDF and PCB, respectively, must be present and must reach their maximum response within two seconds of each other.
For the identification of specific PCB congeners, the retention time of the native congener must be within 0.006 relative retention time (RRT) units of the pre-extraction standard.
If using alternative drying procedures, ensure the cleanliness requirements in Section 13.1 of Method 23 are met.
If using high-purity tank nitrogen, pass it through a bed of activated charcoal approximately 150 mL in volume prior to entering the drying apparatus.

constraints.text: You may reanalyze the sample aliquot or split to reduce the number of isomers to meet the identification criteria in Section 11.4.3.4 of Method 23.
These requirements are achievable using a 30-m narrow bore (0.25 mm ID) 5% phenyl polysilphenylene-siloxane (BPX5 or equivalent) bonded-phase, fused-silica capillary column.

Resolution is shown by a valley between the peaks not exceeding 40% of the taller of the two peaks. Isomers may be unresolved if they have the same TEF and RRF and if these unresolved isomers are uniquely resolved from all other congeners.
Field sampling and recovery staff should be trained and experienced in the best practices for handling and using organic solvents in field environments.
Repeated baking of glassware may cause active sites on the glass surface that may irreversibly adsorb target compounds.
Load the modules in a clean area to avoid contamination.
Clean and enclose the cleanup area to minimize the chances of losing or contaminating the sample.
Smoking or eating in the sample recovery area shall not be allowed to avoid sample contamination and unnecessary exposure to toxic chemicals.
This method does not consider EMPC-flagged data to be zero concentrations.
Include the integrated ion current of both the primary and secondary m/z values for each compound in the calibration standard, excluding PAH, which use only primary m/z values.
The units for Qpe and Qn in Eq. 23–1 and the units for Qpa and Qpe in Equation 23–2 must be the same.
The requirement for the initial calibration RSD is in Section 13.9 and Table 23–14 of this method.
All materials of construction must be glass, PTFE, or stainless steel, and pumps must not contain extractable materials.
The final nitrogen temperature should only be warm to the touch and not over 40 °C.
